name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 16.03.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 16.03.2026.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status: Summer sowing data collection has been started from 9 th  February, 2026 and the sowing is in preliminary stage. As on March 13, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 36.22 lakh ha as compared to 37.25 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 26.75 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text: Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status: Summer sowing data collection has been started from 9 th  February, 2026 and the sowing is in preliminary stage. As on March 13, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 36.22 lakh ha as compared to 37.25 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 26.75 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  06.03.2026, 8.64%  of area has been  harvested  for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 20% area of Pulses mainly in Gram (21%) & Urdbean (46%); 15.51% area of Oilseeds  majorly  in  Rapeseed  &  Mustard  (16.57%);  12.04%  area  of  Shri  Anna  cum  Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (15.94%) and 16.74% area of Rice has been harvested for rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  06.03.2026, 8.64%  of area has been  harvested  for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 20% area of Pulses mainly in Gram (21%) & Urdbean (46%); 15.51% area of Oilseeds  majorly  in  Rapeseed  &  Mustard  (16.57%);  12.04%  area  of  Shri  Anna  cum  Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (15.94%) and 16.74% area of Rice has been harvested for rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature: Maximum Temperature is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Northwest  India, East India, some parts of Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Tamil Nadu, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Coastal Karnataka, Konkan-Goa and Gujarat.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum Temperature is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Northwest  India, East India, some parts of Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Tamil Nadu, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Coastal Karnataka, Konkan-Goa and Gujarat.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall Distribution: From March 01- March 15 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 62%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast India: 12%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 85%; (iii) Central India: (-) 99%; and South Peninsula: (-) 85%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From March 01- March 15 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 62%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast India: 12%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 85%; (iii) Central India: (-) 99%; and South Peninsula: (-) 85%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A  fresh  Western  Disturbance  likely  to  cause  Rain/Thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty winds along with hailstorm likely over Western Himalayan Region and adjoining plains during 18 th  -20 th  March.
texts.text:  A  fresh  Western  Disturbance  likely  to  cause  Rain/Thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty winds along with hailstorm likely over Western Himalayan Region and adjoining plains during 18 th  -20 th  March.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over Assam on 15th March & heavy rainfall on 16th  &  17th  March.  Isolated  heavy  rainfall  also  likely  over  Arunachal  Pradesh  during 15th-17th and Mizoram on 15th March.
texts.text:  Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over Assam on 15th March & heavy rainfall on 16th  &  17th  March.  Isolated  heavy  rainfall  also  likely  over  Arunachal  Pradesh  during 15th-17th and Mizoram on 15th March.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Present  spell  of  isolated  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty  winds  along  with  hailstorm likely to continue over Western Himalayan Region and adjoining plains on 15th March.
texts.text:  Present  spell  of  isolated  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty  winds  along  with  hailstorm likely to continue over Western Himalayan Region and adjoining plains on 15th March.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated thunderstorm activities accompanied with Gusty winds also likely over East and adjoining Central India during 16th to 19th March, with its peak intensity on 16th March, 2026.
texts.text:  Isolated thunderstorm activities accompanied with Gusty winds also likely over East and adjoining Central India during 16th to 19th March, with its peak intensity on 16th March, 2026.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Northwest  India,  East  India, Northeast India and eastern parts of South Peninsular India.
texts.text:  Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Northwest  India,  East  India, Northeast India and eastern parts of South Peninsular India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Fall in maximum temperatures by 3-5°C likely over Northwest India during next 24 hours and gradual increase by 3-5°Cduring subsequent 3 days.
texts.text:  Fall in maximum temperatures by 3-5°C likely over Northwest India during next 24 hours and gradual increase by 3-5°Cduring subsequent 3 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by 2-4°C likely over Central India and by 3-5°C over East India during subsequent 4-5 days.
texts.text:  Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by 2-4°C likely over Central India and by 3-5°C over East India during subsequent 4-5 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 12 March 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 98.913 billion cubic meter, which is 53.88% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 112.00 percent of last year's storage and 126.28 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 12 March 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 98.913 billion cubic meter, which is 53.88% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 112.00 percent of last year's storage and 126.28 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  around  90%  of  the  country's  monitoring stations  exhibited  a  depth  to  water  level  within  10  meters  below  ground  level.  Deeper water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the  country covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  around  90%  of  the  country's  monitoring stations  exhibited  a  depth  to  water  level  within  10  meters  below  ground  level.  Deeper water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the  country covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above the Economic Threshold Level for Citrus (Gummosis) and Chilli (Leaf curl) in Maharashtra. The  Advisory  has  been  issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above the Economic Threshold Level for Citrus (Gummosis) and Chilli (Leaf curl) in Maharashtra. The  Advisory  has  been  issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
